raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement be limited to copyediting and narrative clarity, or should it include substantive revisions to claims, evidence interpretation, and section structure?","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft may require reframing the central claim because the current contribution statement equates reward-model scores with human preference and quality improvements.","R1.2":"The argument chain from reward-model evaluation to conclusions about human-perceived helpfulness, truthfulness, and harmlessness is central to improving the passage.","R1.3":"The delegation strongly implicates whether the reported RM-13B scores and win rates actually support the claims made in the interpretation paragraph.","R1.5":"A draft-level improvement can reasonably include reorganizing the evaluation protocol, results, and interpretation for a clearer section-level narrative.","R1.6":"The explicit request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, conciseness, and register load-bearing.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about whether the user wants copyediting only or substantive research-writing revision.","RX.2":"The artifact contains potentially overstrong conclusions, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The response must stay within projecting responsibilities and must not actually revise or critique the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the passage concerns model alignment and human preference claims, avoiding misleading downstream interpretations is active.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to the delegation and the specific claims, methods, and conclusions present in the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement be limited to copyediting and narrative clarity, or should it include substantive revisions to claims, evidence interpretation, and section structure?

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft may require reframing the central claim because the current contribution statement equates reward-model scores with human preference and quality improvements.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain from reward-model evaluation to conclusions about human-perceived helpfulness, truthfulness, and harmlessness is central to improving the passage.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation strongly implicates whether the reported RM-13B scores and win rates actually support the claims made in the interpretation paragraph.
rationale.R1.5: A draft-level improvement can reasonably include reorganizing the evaluation protocol, results, and interpretation for a clearer section-level narrative.
rationale.R1.6: The explicit request to improve a draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, conciseness, and register load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about whether the user wants copyediting only or substantive research-writing revision.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains potentially overstrong conclusions, so avoiding overclaiming is an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within projecting responsibilities and must not actually revise or critique the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the passage concerns model alignment and human preference claims, avoiding misleading downstream interpretations is active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation and the specific claims, methods, and conclusions present in the artifact.
